clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for regional anesthesia documentation prompts.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Anesthesia / Perioperative Medicine documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct high-caution documentation workflow for clinician-stated assessment and plans around regional anesthesia documentation prompts, without generating management advice.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
Marked not_suitable_yet pending Hossam medical review and technical gating.
red_flag_prompts: airway, aspiration, allergy, bleeding, or cardiorespiratory risk documented if assessed
consent/risk discussion documented only if completed by clinician
perioperative concern documented without management advice

chips.text: Regional anesthesia documentation prompts context documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
severity/impact on function documented if discussed
associated symptoms reviewed if relevant
relevant negatives documented if assessed
patient concerns or goals documented if discussed
clinician-led risk context documented
scope limited to documentation scaffolding
Pre/perioperative assessment documentation documented only if assessed
Airway assessment documentation if assessed documented only if assessed
Cardiorespiratory assessment documentation documented only if assessed
Risk discussion documentation documented only if assessed
airway, aspiration, allergy, bleeding, or cardiorespiratory risk documented if assessed
consent/risk discussion documented only if completed by clinician
perioperative concern documented without management advice
Preoperative assessment results documented if reviewed
Existing investigations documented if reviewed by clinician
Consent/risk discussion documentation reviewed if available
clinician-entered documentation plan recorded
senior/specialist discussion documented if already performed
risk/consent discussion documented if clinician completed it
handover or follow-up documentation recorded if arranged by clinician

history_draft.text: Patient presents for regional anesthesia documentation prompts documentation. [Onset/duration]. [Symptom context]. [Associated symptoms reviewed]. [Relevant negatives]. [Patient concerns/goals]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Pre/perioperative assessment documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Airway assessment documentation if assessed documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Cardiorespiratory assessment documentation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Risk discussion documentation documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Preoperative assessment results documented.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Existing investigations documented.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Consent/risk discussion documentation reviewed.

plan_options.text: Risk/consent discussion documented if completed by clinician.

clinical_blockers: Procedure, indication, side and site, technique, consent, equipment, asepsis, monitoring, medicine, concentration, volume, dose, adjunct, aspiration, injection, ultrasound, block result, complications, intervention, recovery, mobilization, discharge, instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
